channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Enhancing Outcome Measurement in Oncology Clinical Trials Through Artificial Intelligence: A Scoping Review
item.description: This scoping review examines the application of artificial intelligence to enhance outcome measurement in oncology clinical trials. The review synthesizes evidence on how AI can improve the accuracy, efficiency, and standardization of outcome assessment across oncology trial designs and populations.

item.title: Greece unveils national AI act framework: what changes for citizens, businesses and government
item.description: Greece's parliamentary committee has now begun debating the national bill implementing the European Union's AI Act, as Digital Governance Minister Dimitris Papastergiou stresses balancing citizen protection with innovation across healthcare, employment, and p…

item.title: On 2 august 2026, europe’s grace period on its landmark AI law ends and the commission gains the power to fine the makers of the most powerful models — for how they trained them and for what those systems do in the world
item.description: On August 2nd 2026, the European Union’s rulebook for artificial intelligence stops being a set of deadlines on paper and starts having teeth. From that day, the European Commission can supervise the companies that build the most powerful AI models, and fine…

item.title: Openai’s head of safety is leaving the company
item.description: Johannes Heidecke’s departure comes as OpenAI tries to further integrate its research and safety teams.

item.title: Calcium signaling pathway implicates a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases
item.description: A study identifies calcium signaling pathway involvement in a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases, suggesting common molecular mechanisms underlying both disease categories. This finding, derived from analysis of genetic data, may have implications for understanding

item.title: Digital and AI-enabled models in pharmaceutical development and manufacturing: a regulatory-focused industry survey
item.description: This industry survey examines the implementation and regulatory considerations of digital and AI-enabled models across pharmaceutical development and manufacturing processes. The study, conducted by experts from academic institutions and industry, provides insights into current practices and regulat

item.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
item.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

item.title: Application of plant viruses in biotechnology with relation to artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology
item.description: This review examines the application of plant viruses in biotechnology, with emphasis on their integration with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology approaches. The article explores how these emerging technologies can enhance the development and utilization of plant virus-ba

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: Which ‘ai scientist’ suits your lab? a guide for the perplexed
item.description: General purpose AI tools for science, such as Claude Science, promise to accelerate research. But which is right for you?

item.title: Health system learning enables generalist neuroimaging models
item.description: NeuroVFM, trained on routine health system MRI and CT scans, learns general neuroimaging representations that improve diagnosis, report generation and triage, showing how private clinical data will power safer, more accurate medical AI.

item.title: Talk python to me: #554: trustworthy AI in healthcare and longevity
item.description: You ask an AI a question and it answers with total confidence. Most of the time, a confidently wrong answer is just an annoyance. But what if the question is medical, and there's a real patient on the other end? In that world, a hallucination isn't a bug, it'…

item.title: A call to action for an equitable global genomic system
item.description: As genomics infrastructure expands, current governance will determine whether it advances global health equity or entrenches disparities, locking biased data into future clinical tools. This Comment calls for inclusive, community-centered genomic systems, bef…

item.title: Openai says GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for microsoft copilot amid breakup chatter | techcrunch
item.description: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for Microsoft Copilot amid breakup chatter | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: FDA Proposes Rule to Modernize Drug Manufacturing Registration
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a proposed rule that, if finalized, would create a streamlined registration pathway for distributed manufacturing establishments that operate as a single establishment using a “hub-and-spoke” model.

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts The AI firm Anthropic has got the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on ins

item.title: Coffee break: more on american science, thomas jefferson and AI, and natural history for the ages
item.description: Part the First: Beware of Resting on the Shoulders of Atlas. Dr. Scott Atlas is a radiologist who is now ensconced in the Hoover Institution on War, Revolution, and Peace at Stanford (naturally, and in the tower, no less). One can only surmise from the gene…

item.title: AI model mania and the new chip gold rush
item.description: Just when you thought the artificial intelligence model race might slow down, it starts up again double-time. This week alone brought new models and related services from OpenAI (twice), Meta (also twice), SpaceXAI, Anthropic and a raft of Chinese companies. …

item.title: Ai-guided identification of natural ctsl inhibitors with therapeutic potential for renal injury
item.description: Author summary Computational approaches are increasingly used to speed up early-stage drug discovery, but many disease-relevant targets still lack effective inhibitors. Cathepsin L (CTSL) is an enzyme implicated in kidney injury and blocking CTSL has shown ki…

item.title: Preventive health offers new opportunities in private healthcare as patient needs develop: ihh
item.description: Preventive health offers new opportunities in private healthcare as patient needs develop: IHHbusinesstimes.com.sg

item.title: Europe torched its own rulebook to please business, and business is still unhappy
item.description: Twenty months into the EU’s campaign to slash red tape, many of the businesses that demanded it are unimpressed. Firms told Politico the “simplification” drive is too slow, too costly, and too complicated. Politico spoke to 17 companies, consultancies, and tr…

item.title: AI labels could be coming to music streaming platforms soon
item.description: The great battle to rein in the rampant spread of online deepfakes continues.

item.title: Digital banking creates a new kind of financial exclusion
item.description: Rolling out digital banking without addressing algorithmic bias risks excluding the very populations it promises to uplift. Meghna Chotaliya uncovers a financial system that is technologically advanced and socially regressive at the same time and explains why…

item.title: Why mandating and enforcing EU regulation is preferable to a social media ban
item.description: Should the EU ban young people from accessing social media? Elisabeth Staksrud, Giovanna Mascheroni, Brian O’Neill and Sonia Livingstone argue that rather than restricting children’s access to digital life, the … Continued The post Why mandating and enforcing…

item.title: Build a semantic layer for agentic AI on aws with stardog and amazon bedrock agentcore
item.description: In this post we show how to build a semantic layer on AWS using Stardog’s Semantic AI Application over Amazon Aurora and Amazon Redshift, and how to run a Strands Agents agent on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore that queries the layer to answer customer 360 questions…

item.title: Data privacy: your apps may know more about you than your friends do
item.description: As travel becomes increasingly digital, privacy may become a key differentiator between services. Companies that can balance personalization with responsible data stewardship could gain a competitive advantage in an era where trust is becoming as valuable as …

item.title: The covid reckoning that never came
item.description: Over the last several years I have been posting nonstop on X about the same nightmares we’ve been living through…the COVID psyop, the experimental mRNA shots, the mandates that destroyed lives, the injuries, the excess deaths, and the relentless propaganda ma…

item.title: The agentic marketing stack starts with the data layer
item.description: * Acxiom is building an end-to-end agentic marketing value chain — from identity resolution and audience planning to media buying, campaign activation and performance analytics — and the foundation making it possible is a modern, cloud-native data architectur…

item.title: Hiv capsid inhibitors: mechanisms, resistance, and therapeutic advances
item.description: The capsid (CA) of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) has emerged as a critical therapeutic target owing to its essential roles in viral replication, nuclear import, and integration. Capsid inhibitors (CIs) disrupt these processes by binding to highly con…

item.title: Leadership, teamwork and work outcomes in healthcare: a test of a model
item.description: Effective teamwork in healthcare improves patient outcomes, staff well-being, and service delivery. To enhance these benefits, NHS teams should implement targeted leadership training, routinely assess and clarify roles to reduce ambiguity, and encourage staff…

item.title: Transcriptomic meta-analysis identifies dysregulated pathways and potential therapeutic targets in vestibular schwannoma
item.description: Vestibular schwannoma (VS) is a benign Schwann cell–derived tumor that frequently causes progressive hearing loss and vestibulocochlear dysfunction, substantially impacting quality of life. The molecular mechanisms underlying VS pathobiology remain poorly def…

item.title: Biobank-scale genotype similarity search and dynamic patient-matched cohort creation with genosis
item.description: Many patients do not experience optimal benefits from medical advances because clinical research does not adequately represent them. Although the diversity of biomedical research cohorts is improving, ensuring that individual patients are adequately represent…

item.title: Millions who rely on us-funded hiv/aids programs face uncertainty as financial cliff looms
item.description: Millions of HIV/AIDS patients, many of them in Africa, face an uncertain future as a financial cliff-edge for US-funded global programs fast approaches.

item.title: Meta turns off the instagram feature that users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
item.description: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any publi…

item.title: Apple is suing openai for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
item.description: The iPhone-maker claims OpenAI encouraged poached employees to bring over confidential presentations, secret prototypes, and key supplier details.

item.title: Apple sues openai over alleged trade secret theft
item.description: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAi's senior leadership, including a long-time former employee.

item.title: Hugging face's CEO on why companies are done renting their AI | techcrunch
item.description: Watch as Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue joins Equity to talk open source vs closed source, and why he's worried about the possibility that a handful of big companies could end up controlling everything.

item.title: Openai's atlas browser doesn't make it to its first birthday
item.description: Standalone experiment killed after less than 12 months as model maker redirects agentic ambitions towards workplace productivity

item.title: A new experiential gallery just might change your mind about AI art
item.description: Billed as the “world’s first museum of AI arts,” Dataland uses wearables and troves of material from the Amazon to merge nature, biometrics, and art.

item.title: STAT+: The quest to save Grace — and clear the way for rare disease patients everywhere
item.description: How a father’s quest may shape the future of rare disease gene therapy.

item.title: Alchemab lands £25m from british business bank in record life sciences bet
item.description: Cambridge biotech Alchemab Therapeutics has extended its Series A round with a £25 million ($34 million) investment from the British Business Bank, which the company says is the state-owned lender’s largest backing of a life sciences company to date. The mone…

item.title: Biological aging clocks in health and disease
item.description: This review outlines the state of the art of biological clocks and how they could help understand pathobiology, reduce the burden of disease and extend healthspan.

item.title: Courts split over AI training fair use rulings [let's data science]
item.description: U.S. courts remain split on whether training AI models on copyrighted material is fair use, after **Judge William Alsup** ruled in June 2025 that training on purchased, digitized books was fair use while **Judge Vince Chhabria** reached the opposite conclusio…

item.title: Colorado’s admt comment window closes with no industry voice arguing for agent governance
item.description: Colorado’s recent pre-rulemaking comment period for the ADMT Act (SB 26-189) was defined by a conspicuous void. Between June 15 and July 13, 2026, stakeholders had a rare window to shape the regulatory future of automated decision-making. Yet, across the subm…

item.title: Human embryonic stem cell-derived dopaminergic cells for parkinson’s disease: a phase 1/2 open-label trial
item.description: A phase 1/2 trial of an off-the-shelf stem cell-derived dopaminergic progenitor therapy demonstrated a favorable 12-month safety profile, with no graft-related adverse events, dyskinesias or tumor formation, although risks were associated with the immunosuppr…

item.title: 85% of financial firms plan AI budget increases
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence (AI) is no longer just a technology project. It is becoming a line item with momentum. That’s the clearest signal from “The Enterprise AI Benchmark Report: Financial Services Pulls Ahead in the Enterprise AI Race,” a new PYM…

item.title: When the real world becomes the trial
item.description: Once confined to post-market surveillance, real-world evidence is now being used to emulate trials, guide approvals and even run studies in real time, forcing a rethink of what constitutes a clinical trial.

item.title: Anthropic's new "j-lens" reveals a silent workspace inside claude that mirrors a leading theory of consciousness | venturebeat
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude research reveals a hidden internal “global workspace” that resembles human conscious processing, raising major questions about AI reasoning, interpretability, safety, and machine consciousness.

item.title: Molecular design beyond training data with novel extended objective functionals of generative AI models driven by quantum annealing computer
item.description: This study presents a novel approach to molecular design that extends generative AI model capabilities beyond their training data through the use of extended objective functionals combined with quantum annealing computing. The method integrates quantum annealing technology to optimize molecular stru

item.title: Proteome-scale quantification of the interactions driving condensate formation of intrinsically disordered proteins
item.description: This study quantifies protein-protein interactions at the proteome scale that drive condensate formation by intrinsically disordered proteins, providing systematic characterization of the molecular mechanisms underlying phase separation. The research integrates experimental and computational approac
